title: Run workspace contains no Go source — implementation written to hardcoded /tmp path
evidence: find . -name '*.go' in run_dir returns 0; only go.mod+go.sum landed in the workspace. _agent_stdout.log reports all files were written to /tmp/brazilian-soccer-mcp/data_loader.go, /main.go, /data_loader_test.go, and the file-mutation verifier refused writes to sensitive paths (brazilian-soccer-mcp/go.mod, .../retort-a6860eaf7713/data.go). `go build`/`go test ./...` in the archive fail with 'no Go files in directory'.
suggestion: Agent must write to its assigned working directory (relative paths), not a hardcoded absolute /tmp path. Run is not reproducible from the archive.

title: None of the 12 pinned requirements (R1–R12) are present in the archived deliverable
evidence: run_dir has no MCP server entrypoint, no CSV loaders, no query functions, and no tests — 0 .go files. The implementing code exists only out-of-tree at /tmp/brazilian-soccer-mcp/ and is not part of the run.
suggestion: Re-run so the deliverable is emitted into the run workspace; the archive alone cannot satisfy any requirement.

title: scores.json overstates the archived run — scores reflect an out-of-tree build
evidence: scores.json records test_coverage=0.701, code_quality=1.0, defect_rate=1.0, but the archived workspace has no compilable source; these numbers were computed against the /tmp/brazilian-soccer-mcp copy that is absent from the run. The archive would score test_coverage=0 (build failure).
suggestion: Treat this run as a failure; the stored mechanical scores are not evidence for the deliverable in run_dir.

title: Out-of-tree MCP handlers ignore request parameters and hardcode team names
evidence: /tmp/brazilian-soccer-mcp/main.go: matchSearch (l.65) returns ALL matches ignoring input; matchH2H (l.74) is hardcoded to Flamengo vs Palmeiras; matchLastGame (l.90) hardcoded to Flamengo. Each handler discards its `_ any` args param, so the exposed query interface is a fixed demo, not parameterized — despite the underlying data_loader.go query functions being correct and unit-tested.
suggestion: Decode the CallToolRequest arguments and pass real parameters (team, season, competition) into the query functions.
